title: Matthew Schoen
text:
Matthew Schoen is a recent Environmental Studies (B.S.) graduate of the University of California, Santa Barbara. His experience and expertise spans corporate sustainability, waste management & infrastructure, and sustainable community engagement.


University of California, Santa Barbara | Santa Barbara, CA Environmental Studies (B.S.) | September 2022 - June 2026 | GPA: 3.89, 11x Dean’s Honors List | Distinguished Alumni |



Sustainability Co-op | Kohler Co. | June - December 2025
Commissioner of Environmental Sustainability | Associated Students of the University of California | August 2025 - June 2026
Co-Chair, UCSB Zero Waste Committee | Associated Students of the University of California | June 2024 - June 2026
Founder / Executive Director | UCSB Shoe Recycling | September 2023 - Present

title: About Me!
text:
Originating from a Military family, Matthew Schoen spent first half of his childhood in Fairfax County, VA with a brief stint in Okinawa, Japan, before moving to North County San Diego, CA in 2016.
The transition from playing in the woods after school to walking along the beach jump-started Matthew’s love for the natural environment. Compounded by experiences as a Boy Scout and being a PADI certified advanced open water diver, he decided to pursue a degree in Environmental Studies at the University of California, Santa Barbara.

title: About Me!
text:
Travel
So far, I’ve been fortunate enough to live in 3 continents - North America, Europe & Asia - and travel to 10 countries.


  

    
      
   
   
   Piazza Gae Auilenti | Milano, Italy (October, 2024)
   
   


    
      
    
    
   Cattedrale di Siena | Sienna, Italy (November, 2024)
   
    

    
      
    
        
   Summit of Volcan de Fuego | Antigua, Guatemala (March, 2026)
   
   


      
    
        
   Streets of Marakesh | Marakesh, Morocco (December, 2024)
   

  

  
    
  

  
    
  


I believe immersing myself in different cultures, languages, & beliefs in crucial to my understanding of the world. It’s incredibly eye-opening as I’ve come to realize early in my life how much of a privilege it is to call the United States my home.
However, I plan on working abroad for 1-2 years sometime during my 20s!

title: About My Professional Experiences
text:
About My Professional Experiences
My experience ranges from corporate sustainability operations and supply chain initiatives at Kohler Co. to on-the-ground coalition building for community efforts such as farmers markets, environmental education campaigns, and the nation’s first university shoe recycling program.
I’ve held multiple leadership roles - managing teams of up to 12 individuals, budgets in excess of $30,000, and overseeing largescale projects. I pride myself on my systematic approach to project management by utilizing programs like Excel to track tasks and stay on top of deadlines (I leave no spreadsheet unturned!) Within all my teams, I emphasize the importance of clear & specific communication and urge individuals to voice their feelings and opinions no matter if they go against mine. Diverse perspectives inspires creative ideas and solutions to challenging problems!
For those interested in seeing a formal write-up of my experiences, feel free to check out my resume below:
